title: Serving Static Files Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of static files and how to serve them in modern Express.js applications.

questions.question: What is the primary way to serve static files (like images or CSS) in an Express.js application?
options: By writing a separate route for each file.
By using the built-in express.static middleware.
By storing all files in the database.
By only using a CDN.

questions.question: What is a 'Virtual Path Prefix' in the context of express.static?
options: A way to encrypt file names for security.
A mount path for static files that doesn't exist on the file system (e.g., /static).
A type of database indexing.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: Which Node.js module is used to ensure that the file path to a static directory is always correct regardless of where the app is started from?
options: Fs
Http
Path
Crypto

questions.question: What's the benefit of setting 'maxAge' in the express.static options?
options: To make the files expire automatically in the browser's cache.
To automatically resize images for different devices.
To tell the browser how long it should cache a file before asking the server for a new version.
To hide the files from search engines.

questions.question: Why should you isolate your static files into a dedicated directory like 'public'?
options: To keep your code organized and prevent exposing private files by accident.
To make the application faster.
To automatically generate a backup of the data.
To improve the user experience.
